textPlain: Meta’s data center being built in Richland Parish would be over 90% of the size of Boston’s Logan International Airport (#16 in the US by passenger throughput, but 1/2 the throughput of Denver which is #3).  Which to be fair, it’s an extremely compact footprint given Denver Intl is larger than the city of Boston has land area.The 5GW consumption I assume is per day?  If so, that’s on par with 170,000 average homes… being put in a parish with around 7250 households.In terms of the space it takes?  It fits.  Easily.  That’s not an issue.  Yet increasing the power consumption of an area by 25X over a few years requires a significant amount of transmission and distribution infrastructure upgrades to make that happen.  Someone has to pay for it, and apparently a big chunk of that ‘someone’ is everyone else.

I was looking into energy markets and how they work and it is truly a cluster of moving parts all along the Eastern Interconnect. The question is when is the shoe going to really drop? You can only keep prices going up on an inelastic good before something really bad happens, and this doesn't even touch climate tail risks like heat sagging tx lines across the grid.
Would have liked a text version. And that would save energy too.

This is a tangent, but one untapped source of energy savings that seems to be invisible to climate activists is Microsoft Windows' constant drain on resources relative to Linux and MacOS. It's shocking how energy inefficient Windows is even when it's doing absolutely nothing noticable for a user.
